module.label: Pediatric pallor, anemia, bruising, bleeding, or petechiae

use_when: Child or adolescent with pallor, suspected anemia, fatigue with anemia concern, easy bruising, petechiae, purpura, epistaxis, gum bleeding, heavy menstrual bleeding, GI bleeding, hematuria, abnormal CBC, thrombocytopenia, or concern for ITP, iron deficiency, hemolysis, leukemia, lymphoma, or transfusion need.

Pediatric hematology workup needs age-specific safety data, bleeding burden, iron and nutrition risk, hemolysis features, malignancy red flags, individualized bedside maneuvers, CBC and smear interpretation, coagulopathy testing, transfusion thresholds, hematology consultation, and explicit limits for unstable bleeding.
do_not_use_when: Adult patient; use the adult bleeding, anemia, or GI-bleed pathway.

Child has airway compromise, shock, massive hemorrhage, severe altered mental status, suspected sepsis, or intracranial bleeding concern; activate local emergency, transfusion, critical care, or hematology pathway while documenting focused findings.

Primary rash, fever/sepsis, abdominal, urinary, neurologic, trauma, or safeguarding concern dominates; use that pediatric pathway and add this hematology module only when blood count, bruising, petechiae, pallor, bleeding, or transfusion decisions change management.

Known oncology, hemoglobinopathy, congenital bleeding disorder, bone marrow failure, transplant, or chronic transfusion protocol where local hematology guidance overrides this general clinician-support module.

modifier_add_on_policy.rationale: This validated pediatric hematology pack already screens fever, infection recurrence, petechiae with unwell appearance, and malignancy/lymphoma features; generic infection prompts should not replace source-backed heme routing.

requiredQuestions.label: Ask age growth and baseline hematology context
requiredQuestions.text: What is the child's age, growth trajectory, baseline energy, recent weight change, prematurity or chronic disease history, prior CBC pattern, and any known hemoglobinopathy, bleeding disorder, transfusion, oncology, transplant, or hematology follow-up?
requiredQuestions.options: Multi-select: infant / child / adolescent / poor growth or weight loss / chronic disease / known hemoglobinopathy / known bleeding disorder / prior transfusion or hematology care / none known / unable to assess
detail_prompts: Ask current age and whether symptoms differ from baseline.
Ask about growth faltering, weight loss, delayed development, or chronic disease.
Ask about known hemoglobinopathy, bleeding disorder, marrow condition, transfusion history, oncology care, transplant, or hematology plan.
requiredQuestions.when_to_ask: Ask for every pediatric pallor, anemia, bleeding, bruising, petechiae, thrombocytopenia, or abnormal CBC presentation.
requiredQuestions.diagnostic_purpose: Establishes pediatric applicability, baseline risk, chronicity, and whether a local specialty protocol should override this general workup.
requiredQuestions.management_implication: Known hemoglobinopathy, marrow failure, oncology, transplant, chronic transfusion, or abnormal baseline changes thresholds, consultation, and discharge safety.
requiredQuestions.likelihood_ratio_note: Question-level LR+/LR- is not available for baseline pediatric hematology context; it changes applicability, chronicity, and specialty routing.

source.source_section: history and causes of anaemia

requiredQuestions.label: Ask anemia symptoms and instability history
requiredQuestions.text: Are fatigue, pallor, dizziness, syncope, exertional dyspnea, chest pain, palpitations, poor feeding, lethargy, exercise intolerance, or heart failure symptoms present?
requiredQuestions.options: Multi-select: fatigue / pallor / dizziness / syncope / exertional dyspnea / chest pain / palpitations / poor feeding / lethargy / exercise intolerance / none endorsed / unable to assess
detail_prompts: Ask whether symptoms are acute, progressive, exertional, or present at rest.
Ask about syncope, chest pain, palpitations, dyspnea, poor feeding, or lethargy.
Ask caregivers whether pallor or fatigue is new compared with baseline.
requiredQuestions.when_to_ask: Ask whenever pallor, fatigue, abnormal hemoglobin, suspected bleeding, or transfusion need is possible.
requiredQuestions.diagnostic_purpose: Localizes anemia severity and cardiopulmonary strain rather than treating hemoglobin as the only severity marker.
requiredQuestions.management_implication: Syncope, chest pain, dyspnea, poor feeding, lethargy, or heart failure symptoms change monitoring, admission, transfusion discussion, and urgent consultation.
requiredQuestions.likelihood_ratio_note: Question-level LR+/LR- is not available for anemia symptom burden; source guidance uses symptoms with Hb, chronicity, and cause to change management.

source.source_section: RBC transfusion indications and anemia symptoms

requiredQuestions.label: Ask bleeding source and severity history
requiredQuestions.text: Is there epistaxis, gum bleeding, oral bleeding, hematemesis, melena, hematochezia, hematuria, heavy menstrual bleeding, prolonged bleeding after procedures, or recurrent bleeding episodes?
requiredQuestions.options: Multi-select: epistaxis / gum bleeding / oral bleeding / hematemesis / melena / hematochezia / hematuria / heavy menstrual bleeding / prolonged procedure bleeding / recurrent episodes / none endorsed / unable to assess
detail_prompts: Ask current and recent bleeding sites, frequency, duration, and estimated amount.
Ask about GI bleeding, urinary bleeding, menstrual bleeding, and procedure-related bleeding.
Ask whether bleeding is active now or associated with dizziness, syncope, dyspnea, or weakness.
requiredQuestions.when_to_ask: Ask whenever bruising, pallor, anemia, thrombocytopenia, petechiae, purpura, or bleeding concern is present.
requiredQuestions.diagnostic_purpose: Separates mucocutaneous bleeding, GI/GU blood loss, menstrual blood loss, and procedural bleeding patterns that require different tests and actions.
requiredQuestions.management_implication: Active, recurrent, GI/GU, menstrual, or procedure-related bleeding changes coagulation testing, type and screen, transfusion threshold, specialty consultation, and source-specific workup.
requiredQuestions.likelihood_ratio_note: Question-level LR+/LR- is not available for bleeding-source history; management changes by site, severity, hemodynamics, platelet count, and coagulation results.

source.source_section: clinical features and bleeding assessment

requiredQuestions.label: Ask bruising petechiae and trauma pattern history
requiredQuestions.text: Are bruises, petechiae, purpura, non-blanching spots, rapidly spreading lesions, mucosal bleeding, pain, fever, trauma, inconsistent injury story, or mechanical causes such as coughing or vomiting present?
requiredQuestions.options: Multi-select: easy bruising / petechiae / purpura / non-blanching spots / rapidly spreading lesions / mucosal bleeding / fever / trauma / inconsistent story / mechanical coughing or vomiting / none endorsed / unable to assess
detail_prompts: Ask when bruising or petechiae started and whether they are spreading.
Ask about fever, mucosal bleeding, pain, trauma, injury explanation, and mechanical causes.
Ask whether petechiae are above the nipple line after coughing or vomiting or more generalized.
requiredQuestions.when_to_ask: Ask whenever bruising, petechiae, purpura, thrombocytopenia, non-blanching rash, or safeguarding concern is possible.
requiredQuestions.diagnostic_purpose: Separates mechanical or traumatic petechiae from ITP, sepsis, leukemia, vasculitis, coagulopathy, and non-accidental injury patterns.
requiredQuestions.management_implication: Fever, unwell appearance, mucosal bleeding, rapid spread, unexplained bruising, or inconsistent story changes sepsis, hematology, safeguarding, and admission actions.
requiredQuestions.likelihood_ratio_note: Question-level LR+/LR- is not available for bruising and petechiae patterns; guideline-defined danger features and distribution change escalation.

source.source_section: assessment and management of petechiae and purpura

requiredQuestions.label: Ask iron nutrition and blood loss risk history
requiredQuestions.text: Is there excess cow's milk intake, low iron diet, vegetarian or vegan diet, poor intake, pica, prematurity, rapid growth, gastrointestinal symptoms, chronic inflammation, menstrual blood loss, or poor response to prior iron?
requiredQuestions.options: Multi-select: excess cow's milk / low iron diet / vegetarian or vegan diet / poor intake / pica / prematurity / rapid growth / GI symptoms / chronic inflammation / menstrual blood loss / prior iron nonresponse / none endorsed / unable to assess
detail_prompts: Ask milk volume, iron-rich foods, dietary restrictions, pica, and supplement adherence.
Ask about prematurity, rapid growth, chronic inflammation, GI symptoms, or blood loss.
Ask whether prior iron was taken consistently and whether hemoglobin or reticulocytes improved.
requiredQuestions.when_to_ask: Ask when pallor, microcytosis, low ferritin, suspected iron deficiency, fatigue, or chronic anemia is present.
requiredQuestions.diagnostic_purpose: Identifies common pediatric iron-deficiency causes and situations where additional workup is needed.
requiredQuestions.management_implication: Positive risk changes ferritin testing, oral iron and diet plan, dosing, adherence counseling, repeat labs, and referral when response is poor or diagnosis is uncertain.
requiredQuestions.likelihood_ratio_note: Question-level LR+/LR- is not available for iron-risk history; ferritin, CBC indices, inflammation context, and response to iron change management.

source.source_section: risk factors, investigations, and treatment

requiredQuestions.label: Ask hemolysis jaundice and dark urine history
requiredQuestions.text: Are jaundice, scleral icterus, dark urine, episodic pallor, abdominal pain, recent infection, medication exposure, family anemia, gallstones, splenectomy, or ethnic/family hemoglobinopathy risk present?
requiredQuestions.options: Multi-select: jaundice / dark urine / episodic pallor / abdominal pain / recent infection / medication exposure / family anemia / gallstones / splenectomy / hemoglobinopathy risk / none endorsed / unable to assess
detail_prompts: Ask about jaundice, dark urine, episodic symptoms, abdominal pain, and triggers.
Ask about recent infection, oxidant medications, family anemia, gallstones, splenectomy, and ethnicity-linked hemoglobinopathy risk.
Ask whether symptoms are acute or recurrent.
requiredQuestions.when_to_ask: Ask when anemia is normocytic or macrocytic, reticulocyte count is high, bilirubin is elevated, urine is dark, jaundice is visible, or hemolysis is possible.
requiredQuestions.diagnostic_purpose: Routes anemia toward hemolysis, hemoglobinopathy, enzymopathy, immune hemolysis, or hepatobiliary mimics.
requiredQuestions.management_implication: Positive hemolysis features change bilirubin/LDH/haptoglobin/DAT testing, urine testing, transfusion caution, hematology consultation, and admission threshold.
requiredQuestions.likelihood_ratio_note: Question-level LR+/LR- is not available for hemolysis history; lab-confirmed hemolysis and clinical severity change management.

source.source_section: causes and investigations of anaemia

requiredQuestions.label: Ask leukemia lymphoma and marrow red flags
requiredQuestions.text: Are unexplained petechiae, bruising, pallor, persistent fatigue, fever, recurrent infections, weight loss, night sweats, pruritus, bone pain, limp, lymph node swelling, abdominal fullness, or parental concern present?
requiredQuestions.options: Multi-select: unexplained petechiae / unexplained bruising / pallor / persistent fatigue / fever / recurrent infections / weight loss / night sweats / pruritus / bone pain or limp / lymph node swelling / abdominal fullness / parental concern / none endorsed / unable to assess
detail_prompts: Ask about persistent fatigue, fever, recurrent infections, pallor, bruising, petechiae, and bone pain.
Ask about lymph node swelling, abdominal fullness, night sweats, pruritus, weight loss, and parental concern.
Ask whether symptoms are unexplained, persistent, worsening, or grouped together.
requiredQuestions.when_to_ask: Ask whenever pediatric anemia, pallor, bruising, petechiae, lymphadenopathy, splenomegaly, bone pain, or abnormal CBC is present.
requiredQuestions.diagnostic_purpose: Identifies NICE child and young-person leukemia and lymphoma referral triggers.
requiredQuestions.management_implication: Positive unexplained features change CBC urgency, blood film review, immediate or very urgent specialist assessment, imaging, admission, and discharge constraints.
requiredQuestions.likelihood_ratio_note: Question-level LR+/LR- is not available for leukemia/lymphoma red flags; NICE referral thresholds guide urgency.

source.source_section: symptoms and signs in children and young people

requiredQuestions.label: Ask medication anticoagulant and family bleeding history
requiredQuestions.text: Has the child used anticoagulants, antiplatelets, NSAIDs, steroids, chemotherapy, antiepileptics, antibiotics, herbal medicines, toxins, or supplements, and is there family history of heavy bleeding, thrombosis, anemia, early gallstones, or consanguinity?
requiredQuestions.options: Multi-select: anticoagulant / antiplatelet / NSAID / steroid / chemotherapy / antiepileptic / antibiotic / herbal or supplement / toxin exposure / family bleeding / family anemia / early gallstones / consanguinity / none known / unable to assess
detail_prompts: Ask all prescribed, over-the-counter, herbal, and recent medicines.
Ask about anticoagulant, antiplatelet, NSAID, chemotherapy, antibiotic, antiepileptic, toxin, or supplement exposure.
Ask family history of heavy bleeding, anemia, gallstones, thrombosis, or consanguinity.
requiredQuestions.when_to_ask: Ask for every pediatric bleeding, bruising, anemia, thrombocytopenia, or abnormal CBC workup.
requiredQuestions.diagnostic_purpose: Identifies medication-induced bleeding or cytopenia, inherited bleeding disorders, inherited anemia, and hemolysis risk.
requiredQuestions.management_implication: Positive exposure or family history changes medication holds, coagulation testing, hemolysis testing, hematology consultation, and counseling.
requiredQuestions.likelihood_ratio_note: Question-level LR+/LR- is not available for medication and family history; source-guided interpretation depends on CBC, film, coagulation tests, and clinical context.

source.source_section: history and causes of anaemia

conditionalQuestions.label: Ask adolescent confidential bleeding and pregnancy context
conditionalQuestions.text: For an adolescent, are heavy or prolonged periods, intermenstrual bleeding, possible pregnancy, postpartum state, sexual activity, eating disorder concern, self-harm, substance use, or confidential safety concerns present?
conditionalQuestions.options: Multi-select: heavy periods / prolonged periods / intermenstrual bleeding / possible pregnancy / postpartum / sexual activity / eating disorder concern / self-harm concern / substance use / safety concern / all denied / unable to ask privately
detail_prompts: Ask privately about menstrual bleeding duration, pad/tampon burden, flooding, clots, and anemia symptoms.
Ask pregnancy possibility and postpartum state when clinically applicable.
Ask confidentially about self-harm, substance use, eating disorder concern, and safety.
conditionalQuestions.when_to_ask: Ask privately when the patient is an adolescent, postmenarchal, pregnancy-capable, has heavy menstrual bleeding, unexplained anemia, or safety concerns.
conditionalQuestions.diagnostic_purpose: Separates menstrual blood loss, pregnancy/postpartum complications, nutritional restriction, self-harm, and safety contexts.
conditionalQuestions.management_implication: Positive findings change pregnancy testing, gynecology consultation, iron therapy, bleeding treatment, safeguarding, and confidential follow-up.
conditionalQuestions.trigger: adolescent teen heavy period menstruation menstrual pregnancy postpartum self harm eating disorder
conditionalQuestions.likelihood_ratio_note: Question-level LR+/LR- is not available for adolescent confidential context; it changes safety, pregnancy, gynecology, and bleeding management.

source.source_section: history and causes of anaemia

conditionalQuestions.label: Ask ITP exclusion and low-risk context
conditionalQuestions.text: If ITP is suspected, is the child otherwise well, with no fever, bone pain, weight loss, lymphadenopathy, hepatosplenomegaly, abnormal film, anemia, neutropenia, major bleeding, medication trigger, or safeguarding concern?
conditionalQuestions.options: Multi-select: otherwise well / fever / bone pain / weight loss / lymphadenopathy / hepatosplenomegaly / abnormal film / anemia / neutropenia / major bleeding / medication trigger / safeguarding concern / unable to assess
detail_prompts: Ask whether the child is well between bruising or petechiae episodes.
Ask for ITP exclusions: systemic symptoms, abnormal film, additional cytopenias, organomegaly, lymph nodes, medication triggers, and major bleeding.
Ask whether any safeguarding concern or inconsistent injury history exists.
conditionalQuestions.when_to_ask: Ask when thrombocytopenia, petechiae, purpura, or bruising suggests possible ITP.
conditionalQuestions.diagnostic_purpose: Prevents an ITP label from being applied to children with alternative-diagnosis features.
conditionalQuestions.management_implication: Any exclusion feature changes from observation-focused ITP care toward urgent senior review, hematology consultation, additional labs, admission, or safeguarding route.
conditionalQuestions.trigger: itp thrombocytopenia low platelets platelet count isolated thrombocytopenia
conditionalQuestions.likelihood_ratio_note: Question-level LR+/LR- is not available for ITP exclusions; RCH defines ITP by isolated thrombocytopenia in a well child with otherwise normal FBE and film.

source.source_section: assessment and differential diagnosis

conditionalQuestions.label: Ask transfusion and specialty protocol context
conditionalQuestions.text: Is there chronic transfusion, thalassemia, sickle cell disease, congenital anemia, congenital heart disease, chemotherapy, transplant, irradiated-product need, prior transfusion reaction, or family refusal or consent barrier?
conditionalQuestions.options: Multi-select: chronic transfusion / thalassemia / sickle cell disease / congenital anemia / congenital heart disease / chemotherapy / transplant / irradiated product need / prior transfusion reaction / consent barrier / none known / unable to assess
detail_prompts: Ask about chronic transfusion program, hemoglobinopathy, congenital anemia, cardiac disease, chemotherapy, transplant, or irradiated-product need.
Ask about prior transfusion reaction, blood-product restrictions, consent barriers, and local hematology plan.
Ask whether a specialty protocol already defines thresholds or product choice.
conditionalQuestions.when_to_ask: Ask when transfusion, severe anemia, chronic anemia, hemoglobinopathy, oncology, transplant, or blood-product prescription is possible.
conditionalQuestions.diagnostic_purpose: Identifies situations where general RBC thresholds are modified by disease, product requirements, or local hematology protocol.
conditionalQuestions.management_implication: Positive context changes transfusion threshold, product selection, consent, hematology consultation, and whether this module should defer to local specialty guidance.
conditionalQuestions.trigger: transfusion hemoglobinopathy thalassemia sickle congenital heart chemotherapy transplant irradiated product
conditionalQuestions.likelihood_ratio_note: Question-level LR+/LR- is not available for transfusion protocol context; it changes management thresholds and product safety.

source.source_section: blood product prescription and special patient groups

requiredExam.label: Inspect conjunctival pallor
requiredExam.technique: Use ambient light or a penlight to inspect lower palpebral conjunctiva while avoiding pressure on the eye; compare with mucosa and clinical context.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when pallor, anemia, fatigue, bleeding, hemolysis, or abnormal CBC is being evaluated.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: Visible anemia clue, jaundice clue, and need to correlate with hemoglobin and hemolysis testing.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated pediatric heme sources; interpret pallor with CBC and clinical severity.
requiredExam.management_change: Marked pallor or jaundice changes urgency of CBC, hemolysis testing, transfusion discussion, admission, and specialty consultation.
requiredExam.limitations: Lighting, baseline skin tone, crying, and examiner variability limit accuracy; a normal look does not exclude anemia.

source.source_section: clinical features and investigations

requiredExam.label: Inspect petechiae distribution
requiredExam.technique: Inspect visible skin for petechiae, purpura, bruises, lesion size, distribution above or below the nipple line, dependent areas, pressure areas, and whether lesions blanch with gentle pressure.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when bruising, petechiae, purpura, thrombocytopenia, fever, bleeding, or non-blanching rash is possible.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: Mechanical petechiae, ITP, sepsis, meningococcal disease, leukemia, vasculitis, coagulopathy, trauma, or safeguarding concern.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available; distribution and unwell appearance change guideline-defined escalation.
requiredExam.management_change: Generalized, non-blanching, purpuric, rapidly spreading, or unexplained lesions change sepsis, CBC/film, hematology, observation, admission, and safeguarding decisions.
requiredExam.equipment_needed: good lighting and clear tumbler or gentle pressure
requiredExam.limitations: A partial skin view can miss dependent or covered lesions; privacy and safeguarding sensitivity are important.

source.source_section: definitions and assessment

requiredExam.label: Inspect oral mucosal bleeding
requiredExam.technique: Inspect lips, gums, buccal mucosa, palate, and tongue for petechiae, wet purpura, gum bleeding, ulcers, pallor, or trauma using a light when needed.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when thrombocytopenia, ITP, bleeding, bruising, petechiae, anticoagulant exposure, or coagulopathy concern is present.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: Clinically important mucosal bleeding, ITP severity, coagulopathy, trauma, infection, or leukemia/chemotherapy mucosal disease.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available; mucosal bleeding changes treatment and observation decisions in source guidance.
requiredExam.management_change: Wet purpura or active mucosal bleeding changes hematology consultation, treatment consideration, admission, platelet/bleeding support, and activity restrictions.
requiredExam.limitations: Young children may not tolerate oral inspection; avoid provoking bleeding.

source.source_section: clinical features and management

requiredExam.label: Palpate cervical lymph nodes
requiredExam.technique: Palpate anterior and posterior cervical chains gently and document size, tenderness, mobility, firmness, fixation, and whether nodes are localized or generalized by history and targeted exam.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when pallor, fatigue, bruising, petechiae, fever, infection recurrence, weight loss, night sweats, pruritus, or lymphoma/leukemia concern is present.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: Reactive lymphadenopathy, leukemia, lymphoma, infection, inflammatory disease, or malignancy referral trigger.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available; NICE referral guidance uses unexplained lymphadenopathy and associated symptoms to guide urgency.
requiredExam.management_change: Unexplained, generalized, firm, fixed, supraclavicular, or systemic-symptom-associated nodes change CBC urgency, imaging/referral consideration, and discharge safety.
requiredExam.limitations: Small reactive nodes are common; interpret with duration, distribution, fever, weight loss, night sweats, pruritus, and CBC/film.

source.source_section: lymphadenopathy and associated symptoms in children and young people

requiredExam.label: Palpate liver edge
requiredExam.technique: With the child supine and relaxed, palpate from the right lower abdomen toward the costal margin during inspiration and document liver edge distance, tenderness, and consistency.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when anemia, hemolysis, leukemia/lymphoma concern, abdominal fullness, jaundice, or systemic symptoms are present.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: Hepatomegaly from hemolysis, infection, leukemia, lymphoma, liver disease, heart failure, or storage/infiltrative disease.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available; hepatomegaly changes red-flag and referral routing when paired with heme symptoms.
requiredExam.management_change: Hepatomegaly changes CBC/film, hemolysis/liver testing, imaging consideration, hematology/oncology referral, admission, and transfusion safety.
requiredExam.limitations: Body habitus, crying, distension, and examiner variability affect palpation; ultrasound may be needed when exam is uncertain and management depends on size.

source.source_section: splenomegaly and associated symptoms in children and young people

requiredExam.label: Palpate spleen tip
requiredExam.technique: With the child supine or right lateral if needed, palpate from the right lower abdomen toward the left costal margin during inspiration and document whether a spleen tip or enlargement is felt.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when anemia, hemolysis, recurrent infection, lymphadenopathy, weight loss, night sweats, pruritus, abdominal fullness, or malignancy concern is present.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: Splenomegaly from hemolysis, leukemia, lymphoma, infection, portal hypertension, storage disease, or hemoglobinopathy.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available; splenomegaly with symptoms is a source-defined referral and escalation cue.
requiredExam.management_change: Splenomegaly changes CBC/film urgency, hemolysis workup, lymphoma/leukemia referral, imaging consideration, activity precautions, admission, and transfusion planning.
requiredExam.limitations: A nonpalpable spleen does not exclude pathology; body habitus and cooperation limit sensitivity.

source.source_section: splenomegaly and associated symptoms in children and young people

requiredExam.label: Measure temperature
requiredExam.management_change: Fever changes source-directed infection testing, sepsis pathway, hematology referral urgency, and discharge safety.
requiredExam.options: ___ C/F / febrile / afebrile / hypothermic / not available
requiredExam.limitations: Antipyretics, timing, immunosuppression, or severe illness can mask fever.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: LR is not applicable to temperature as baseline clinical data.

source.source_section: assessment and serious bacterial infection risk
source.implementation_notes: Keep temperature in baseline data, not physical exam.

requiredExam.technique: Measure temperature using an appropriate bedside thermometer route.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Pediatric pallor, anemia, bruising, bleeding, or petechiae is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: Fever with petechiae, purpura, bruising, pallor, infection recurrence, lymphadenopathy, or splenomegaly changes sepsis, leukemia, lymphoma, and admission urgency.

requiredExam.label: Measure heart rate
requiredExam.action: Measure heart rate and blood pressure with age-appropriate cuff and pediatric interpretation.
requiredExam.management_change: Abnormal rate or BP changes resuscitation, IV access, type and screen, transfusion discussion, observation interval, admission, and escalation.
requiredExam.limitations: Pain, fever, anxiety, dehydration, cuff size, chronic anemia, and active bleeding affect interpretation.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: LR is not applicable to vital signs as baseline clinical data.

source.source_section: red flags and monitoring
source.implementation_notes: Tachycardia can indicate severe anemia or ongoing blood loss.

requiredExam.technique: Palpate or monitor heart rate for a full interval, noting rate and regularity, with pediatric age-band interpretation.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Pediatric pallor, anemia, bruising, bleeding, or petechiae is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: Tachycardia, hypotension, postural symptoms, hypertension, or shock changes bleeding, severe anemia, sepsis, and transfusion urgency.

requiredExam.label: Measure blood pressure
requiredExam.action: Measure heart rate and blood pressure with age-appropriate cuff and pediatric interpretation.
requiredExam.management_change: Abnormal rate or BP changes resuscitation, IV access, type and screen, transfusion discussion, observation interval, admission, and escalation.
requiredExam.limitations: Pain, fever, anxiety, dehydration, cuff size, chronic anemia, and active bleeding affect interpretation.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: LR is not applicable to vital signs as baseline clinical data.

source.source_section: red flags and monitoring
source.implementation_notes: Tachycardia can indicate severe anemia or ongoing blood loss.

requiredExam.technique: Measure blood pressure using an age-appropriate cuff.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Pediatric pallor, anemia, bruising, bleeding, or petechiae is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: Tachycardia, hypotension, postural symptoms, hypertension, or shock changes bleeding, severe anemia, sepsis, and transfusion urgency.

requiredExam.label: Measure respiratory rate
requiredExam.action: Measure respiratory rate, oxygen saturation, and current oxygen delivery.
requiredExam.management_change: Abnormal respiratory data changes oxygen support, transfusion urgency, chest imaging consideration, specialty referral, and monitoring level.
requiredExam.limitations: Crying, fever, pain, asthma, infection, and severe anemia affect respiratory rate and oxygen demand.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: LR is not applicable to respiratory rate or oxygen saturation as baseline clinical data.

source.source_section: shortness of breath with lymphadenopathy or splenomegaly in children
source.implementation_notes: Shortness of breath with lymphadenopathy or splenomegaly changes lymphoma referral urgency.

requiredExam.technique: Count respiratory rate over a full interval, noting rate and effort, with pediatric age-band interpretation.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Pediatric pallor, anemia, bruising, bleeding, or petechiae is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: Dyspnea, tachypnea, hypoxemia, or shortness of breath with lymphadenopathy or splenomegaly changes anemia severity, cardiopulmonary strain, lymphoma concern, and admission urgency.

requiredExam.label: Measure oxygen saturation
requiredExam.action: Measure respiratory rate, oxygen saturation, and current oxygen delivery.
requiredExam.management_change: Abnormal respiratory data changes oxygen support, transfusion urgency, chest imaging consideration, specialty referral, and monitoring level.
requiredExam.limitations: Crying, fever, pain, asthma, infection, and severe anemia affect respiratory rate and oxygen demand.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: LR is not applicable to respiratory rate or oxygen saturation as baseline clinical data.

source.source_section: shortness of breath with lymphadenopathy or splenomegaly in children
source.implementation_notes: Shortness of breath with lymphadenopathy or splenomegaly changes lymphoma referral urgency.

requiredExam.technique: Measure oxygen saturation by pulse oximetry and note current oxygen delivery.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Pediatric pallor, anemia, bruising, bleeding, or petechiae is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: Dyspnea, tachypnea, hypoxemia, or shortness of breath with lymphadenopathy or splenomegaly changes anemia severity, cardiopulmonary strain, lymphoma concern, and admission urgency.

requiredExam.label: Measure current weight
requiredExam.action: Document current measured or best estimated weight in kg before medication, iron, blood-product, or emergency dosing decisions.
requiredExam.management_change: Missing weight blocks reliable iron dosing, transfusion volume planning, and weight-based emergency management.
requiredExam.options: Measured ___ kg / estimated ___ kg / recent reliable weight ___ kg / not available
requiredExam.limitations: Critical illness or immobility may require estimated weight and later confirmation.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: LR is not applicable to weight as baseline clinical data.

source.source_section: oral iron supplementation dosing
source.implementation_notes: Weight belongs in baseline data, not physical exam maneuvers.

requiredExam.technique: Measure current weight using a calibrated scale.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Pediatric pallor, anemia, bruising, bleeding, or petechiae is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: Pediatric iron therapy, transfusion volume, emergency medicine, and product prescription depend on weight.

requiredExam.label: Determine active bleeding burden and mental status
requiredExam.action: Document current bleeding site, estimated burden, recurrent episodes, hemodynamic symptoms, and AVPU or age-appropriate mental status.
requiredExam.management_change: Active heavy bleeding or altered state changes IV access, labs, type and screen, transfusion, resuscitation, observation, and admission.
requiredExam.options: No active bleeding / mild mucocutaneous bleeding / heavy or recurrent bleeding / suspected internal bleeding / AVPU alert / altered / unable to assess
requiredExam.limitations: Bleeding volume estimates can be inaccurate; caregiver report, pads/tissues, stool or emesis description, and serial vitals matter.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: LR is not applicable to active bleeding burden or mental status as baseline safety data.

source.source_section: clinical features and treatment based on bleeding features
source.implementation_notes: Treatment decisions in ITP and bleeding presentations depend on clinical bleeding features, not platelet count alone.

requiredExam.technique: Determine active bleeding burden and mental status through direct bedside assessment.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Pediatric pallor, anemia, bruising, bleeding, or petechiae is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: Active bleeding, syncope, altered state, or heavy ongoing losses change immediate stabilization, transfusion, reversal, and specialty escalation.

conditionalExam.label: Inspect anterior nares
conditionalExam.technique: Inspect the anterior nares with light for active bleeding, clot, crusting, trauma, septal lesion, or unilateral source when epistaxis is reported.
conditionalExam.when_to_perform: Add when epistaxis, recurrent nosebleeds, anticoagulant exposure, thrombocytopenia, or active bleeding is reported.
conditionalExam.diagnostic_target: Epistaxis source, local trauma, mucosal bleeding, thrombocytopenia severity, or coagulopathy.
conditionalExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available for anterior nares inspection; active bleeding changes immediate management.
conditionalExam.management_change: Active bleeding changes local pressure/topical therapy, labs, transfusion or hemostasis support, ENT/hematology consultation, and observation.
conditionalExam.limitations: Posterior bleeding can be missed; do not delay resuscitation for heavy bleeding.

source.source_section: bleeding features and management

conditionalExam.label: Palpate axillary lymph nodes
conditionalExam.technique: Palpate each axilla with the child's arm relaxed and document node size, tenderness, mobility, firmness, fixation, and symmetry.
conditionalExam.when_to_perform: Add when cervical nodes are abnormal, generalized lymphadenopathy is suspected, or systemic leukemia/lymphoma features are present.
conditionalExam.diagnostic_target: Generalized lymphadenopathy, lymphoma, leukemia, infection, or inflammatory disease.
conditionalExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available; generalized lymphadenopathy and systemic features change referral urgency.
conditionalExam.management_change: Axillary lymphadenopathy with systemic features changes CBC/film, imaging, specialist referral, and discharge safety.
conditionalExam.limitations: Reactive nodes are common; interpret with distribution, duration, symptoms, and CBC/film.
conditionalExam.trigger: lymphadenopathy generalized nodes lymphoma leukemia night sweats weight loss pruritus

source.source_section: lymphadenopathy in children and young people

conditionalExam.label: Palpate inguinal lymph nodes
conditionalExam.technique: Palpate inguinal regions when clinically appropriate and document node size, tenderness, mobility, firmness, fixation, and whether findings are localized or generalized.
conditionalExam.when_to_perform: Add when generalized lymphadenopathy, lower-extremity infection, lymphoma/leukemia concern, or systemic symptoms are present.
conditionalExam.diagnostic_target: Generalized lymphadenopathy, local infection, lymphoma, leukemia, or inflammatory disease.
conditionalExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available; generalized lymphadenopathy and systemic features change referral urgency.
conditionalExam.management_change: Inguinal lymphadenopathy with generalized or systemic findings changes CBC/film, referral, imaging consideration, and discharge safety.
conditionalExam.limitations: Respect privacy and chaperone needs; local infection and reactive nodes are common.
conditionalExam.trigger: lymphadenopathy generalized nodes lymphoma leukemia leg infection systemic

source.source_section: lymphadenopathy in children and young people

conditionalExam.label: Inspect target joint swelling
conditionalExam.technique: Inspect the symptomatic joint for swelling, warmth, bruising, deformity, range-limiting pain, and visible hemarthrosis pattern before palpation or movement.
conditionalExam.when_to_perform: Add when joint pain, swelling, hemarthrosis concern, trauma, bleeding disorder, anticoagulant exposure, leukemia, or bone pain is reported.
conditionalExam.diagnostic_target: Hemarthrosis, trauma, septic arthritis, leukemia-related bone pain, inflammatory arthritis, or safeguarding concern.
conditionalExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available; joint findings change bleeding-disorder, infection, trauma, and malignancy routing.
conditionalExam.management_change: Joint swelling or severe pain changes coagulation testing, imaging, orthopedic/infection route, hematology consultation, analgesia, and activity precautions.
conditionalExam.limitations: This is not a full MSK workup; use pediatric MSK pathway if limp, hot joint, non-weight-bearing, or trauma dominates.
conditionalExam.trigger: joint swelling hemarthrosis bone pain limp trauma bleeding disorder anticoagulant

source.source_section: bone pain and leukemia in children and young people

clinical_cutoff_review.review_note: Cutoff rows were added because the prior module evidence did not expose enough explicit guideline thresholds for pathway traversal.
